claim: Cross-check confirms explorer claim: YOLO demand evidence dispatch API implements harness boundary audit for full-tool Claude agents via file snapshot diffing before/after agent execution, with violations blocking PRD readiness. All verified implementation details match explorer report.

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot() creates SHA256 digests of all project files excluding allowed artifact roots, matching explorer claim.
evidence.why: Independent read confirms the core boundary audit mechanism exists as described.

evidence.summary: diffBoundarySnapshots() compares before/after digests to detect added, modified, or deleted files, matching explorer claim.
evidence.why: Independent read confirms the change detection mechanism exists as described.

evidence.summary: Runtime calls buildBoundarySnapshot before/after agents, then converts violations to BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blockers added to readiness, matching explorer claim.
evidence.why: Independent read confirms the enforcement integration exists as described.

evidence.summary: execution_policy declares agent_tool_profile with project_mutation forbidden and allowed_write_roots restricted to artifact output directory, matching explorer claim.
evidence.why: Independent read confirms the policy contract exists as described.

evidence.summary: buildDemandEvidenceAgentPrompt() includes explicit hard rules about boundary control, forbidden writes, and allowed artifact root, matching explorer claim.
evidence.why: Independent read confirms the prompt contract exists as described.

evidence.summary: executionConfig() normalizes agent_tool_profile to boundary/research/full for Claude provider, with full mode requiring allowFullAgentTools flag, matching explorer claim.
evidence.why: Independent read confirms the profile normalization exists as described.

evidence.summary: buildProviderInvocation() accepts claude_allowed_tools and claude_disallowed_tools parameters but executionConfig() sets them to empty strings for evidence agents, matching explorer claim.
evidence.why: Independent read confirms tool restrictions are not used for safety; enforcement is via prompt contract and boundary audit.

evidence.summary: Test boundary blocks project file mutation even when agent results pass, status blocked, boundary.project_mutation violated, matching explorer claim.
evidence.why: Independent read confirms boundary violations override successful agent results and block readiness.

evidence.summary: Test git-backed boundary detects untracked project mutations including files added during agent execution, matching explorer claim.
evidence.why: Independent read confirms boundary audit works with git tracking and catches untracked file additions.

evidence.summary: Test git-backed boundary detects gitignored project mutations outside excluded dirs like .env files, matching explorer claim.
evidence.why: Independent read confirms boundary audit detects mutations to gitignored files that could be secrets or config.

evidence.summary: Test non-git boundary fallback detects modified project files and ignores writes to allowed artifact roots, matching explorer claim.
evidence.why: Independent read confirms boundary audit works for non-git repos and respects allowed write roots.

evidence.summary: Test claude full tool profile requires explicit allowFullAgentTools authorization, defaults to boundary mode without flag, matching explorer claim.
evidence.why: Independent read confirms full-tool semantic mode requires explicit user authorization and is not default.

evidence.summary: Test execute mode requires explicit agent dispatch authorization, dry-run is default, blocked without allowAgentDispatch, matching explorer claim.
evidence.why: Independent read confirms the dispatch defaults to safe dry-run mode and requires explicit authorization for live agent execution.

evidence.summary: DEMAND_EVIDENCE_AGENT_PROTOCOLS define explorer, cross-checker, verifier roles with writes_code=false and cross_checker required_when cross_check, matching explorer claim.
evidence.why: Independent read confirms the protocol contract exists as described.

evidence.summary: evidenceResultBlockingIssues() and projectEvidenceBlockingIssues() convert missing data, clarify/block recommendations, and insufficient project evidence to readiness blockers, matching explorer claim.
evidence.why: Independent read confirms evidence agent results feed into PRD readiness and can keep demand blocked.

evidence.summary: Test cross-check PRD readiness requires every evidence agent result, incomplete agent results block readiness, matching explorer claim.
evidence.why: Independent read confirms router enforces evidence agent completion requirements.

evidence.summary: Test evidence conflicts and existing blockers prevent PRD readiness, agents must agree, matching explorer claim.
evidence.why: Independent read confirms agent conflicts are preserved as blockers rather than averaged away.

evidence.summary: Test external-only evidence cannot satisfy existing project facts, requires project-scoped evidence with concrete locators, matching explorer claim.
evidence.why: Independent read confirms evidence scope validation and the requirement for project evidence with file paths.

evidence.summary: Test evidence records must declare valid scope explicitly, missing scope blocks readiness, matching explorer claim.
evidence.why: Independent read confirms router enforces scope declaration requirements on evidence records.

evidence.summary: Doctrine specifies dispatch does not use tool restrictions for safety; real safety comes from prompt contract, artifact root, boundary snapshots, and readiness blockers, matching explorer claim.
evidence.why: Independent read confirms the product specification documents the security model and audit approach.

evidence.summary: src/demand/evidence-dispatch.ts and src/demand/router.ts are untracked files in the working tree, matching explorer claim.
evidence.why: Independent git status check confirms the evidence dispatch implementation exists but is not yet committed to git.

assumptions: The boundary snapshot SHA256 digest approach is cryptographically sufficient to detect content changes.
Git ls-files -co --exclude-standard -z reliably captures untracked and modified files for boundary audit.
Full-tool Claude agents will honor the prompt contract boundary instructions despite having file write tools available.
The excluded directory list .git, node_modules, dist, coverage, .next, .cache covers all cases where mutations should be ignored.
The 480-second default timeout is sufficient for evidence agents to complete their analysis.
Claude provider without --allowedTools restrictions but with prompt contract is safer than tool restrictions that agents might bypass.
The evidence dispatch implementation in untracked files represents the current working state that will be committed.
risks: If boundary snapshot has a bug that misses certain file patterns, agents could mutate project files undetected.
If prompt injection or tool use error causes agents to ignore boundary instructions, project files could be corrupted.
If the harness crashes between before and after snapshots, boundary violations may not be detected or recorded.
If full-tool agents are used without proper user authorization awareness, users may not understand the risk profile.
If git repos have unusual .gitignore patterns or large binary files, boundary snapshot performance may degrade or produce false positives.
If evidence agents return malformed JSON or hang indefinitely, the dispatch may not complete the boundary audit.
If project files are modified concurrently by another process during dispatch, false positive boundary violations could occur.
If the cross-checker agent itself has access to full tools and ignores its own boundary contract, it could corrupt project files while verifying the boundary system.
missing: Real-world stress testing with actual Claude -p agents using full tools on dirty working trees.
Performance benchmarks for boundary snapshot on large codebases with 10K+ files.
Evidence of boundary audit handling concurrent file modifications by other processes.
Verification that prompt contract boundary instructions are actually followed by Claude in full-tool mode.
Evidence that git ls-files behavior matches walkFiles() fallback for non-git repos.
Recovery procedures for when boundary audit detects violations after agents have already returned results.
Documentation of what happens when dispatch is interrupted or crashes before after snapshot.
Evidence that the cross-checker agent itself respects boundary constraints while challenging other agents' claims.
Verification that untracked implementation files represent the latest committed state or if there are divergent changes.

result.notes: Cross-check confirms all explorer claims with independent reads. Implementation exists with boundary audit via snapshot diffing. Tests show violations block PRD readiness. No conflicts or contradictions found. Missing real-world stress testing with actual Claude agents in dirty working trees justifies final verifier stage.
